observe: Not every zone up here behaves the same. Code holds still. Data moves. Know which floor you're standing on.
fault: You tried to write into the code zone. That floor doesn't allow that — you're in the wrong segment for what you're trying to do.
repair: Match the action to the segment. Code, data, stack — each has its own rules.
escape: Stay in the right segment. I can't protect you from the wrong zone's rules.

observe: Segmentation divides memory into logically meaningful segments.
fault: An operation violated the access rules of its segment.
repair: Match each operation to its correct segment.
escape: Confirm all operations respect segment boundaries.

hints: Code segments are typically read/execute only — writes belong in the data segment.
